name: Valles de Mirva y Rabanal – circular desde Burón (Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre)
region_intro: Hiking around Burón offers diverse landscapes within the Riaño Mountains, situated on the southern slopes of the Cantabrian Mountains in León, Spain. The region features extensive beech forests, such as those found along routes connecting Burón to Retuerto, and includes natural areas like the Mirva Valley. Glacial valleys and varied terrain provide a range of experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Burón The most popular hiking route is Pico Yordas Loop Trail, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route involves significant elevation gain and offers panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valles de Mirva y Rabanal – circular desde Burón (Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre), a moderate 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This circular route connects the Mirva and Rabanal valleys, traversing varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Monte Ranedo Trail, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through diverse forests and meadows, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Burón is defined by the dramatic Riaño Mountains, ancient beech forests, and glacial valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Burón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1200 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Burón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Burón?
A: Burón offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 170 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Burón?
A: Hikers in Burón will discover diverse landscapes within the stunning Riaño Mountains, part of the Cantabrian Mountains. The region features extensive beech forests, such as those found along routes connecting Burón to Retuerto, and includes natural areas like the Mirva Valley. You'll also encounter glacial valleys and panoramic views of the majestic ranges and the expansive Riaño reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Burón has 23 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050121631/el-saltadero-from-acebedo-circular-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltadero from Acebedo – circular Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 4 km long and offers a pleasant, accessible walk.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirva Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041112469/valles-de-mirva-y-rabanal-circular-desde-buron-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valles de Mirva y Rabanal circular route&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the Riaño reservoir and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Burón?
A: Yes, Burón offers several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041112469/valles-de-mirva-y-rabanal-circular-desde-buron-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valles de Mirva y Rabanal – circular desde Burón&lt;/a&gt;, which connects the Mirva and Rabanal valleys. For a more challenging circular hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050141394/pena-ten-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ten Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Burón?
A: The beech forests in Burón are particularly vibrant in autumn, showcasing a spectacular display of colors, making it an ideal time for scenic walks. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions with lush greenery, while late spring to early autumn generally provides the most stable weather for mountain hiking.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Burón?
A: The trails in Burón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1200 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountain scenery, the beauty of the ancient beech forests, and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, there are 68 difficult trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050099424/pico-yordas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Yordas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 18.2 km route with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Can I find trails that go through the famous beech forests?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful beech forests. The Burón to Retuerto trail, mentioned in regional research, traverses one of the most beautiful beech forests in the Riaño Mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050107208/monte-ranedo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Ranedo Trail&lt;/a&gt; also winds through diverse forests and meadows, providing scenic views.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Burón?
A: While specific establishments aren't detailed in the guide data, the region is a popular tourist destination. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050107208/monte-ranedo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Ranedo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads to a traditional hut, suggesting local amenities. For broader options, you'll find cafes, pubs, and accommodation in the village of Burón and surrounding areas.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on the trails in Burón?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Burón are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock. It's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions before heading out with your dog.
tips:
The trail between Burón and Retuerto, passing through Mirva, is part of a beautiful hiking route in the Riaño and Mampodre Mountains, in the province of León.
Beech forests, meadows, and glacial valleys.
Panoramic views of the central and western Picos de Europa mountain ranges.
Retuerto, a charming little town with plenty of opportunities for relaxation.
The Burón to Retuerto Beech Forest Trail is a hiking route that crosses one of the most beautiful beech forests in the Riaño Mountains, in the province of León.

Beautiful panoramic view of the western and central massifs of the Picos de Europa. Down in the valley, we will see the town of Retuerto. About 100 meters away there is a forest hut that also offers us great views.

waypoints.name: Burón–Retuerto Trail through the Beech Forest
View of the Picos de Europa and the Retuerto Valley
